title: lichen_native_init is not idempotent and can spawn duplicate RX threads
description: Why this issue exists: lichen_native_init always overwrites global callback state and creates a new native_rx thread, with no initialized guard. Repeated init calls create multiple threads consuming the same UART message queue and racing on global callback state. What needs to be done: add an initialization guard, return 0 on repeated init with the same callback, and define behavior for callback replacement.
close_reason: Made lichen_native_init idempotent: repeated calls update the callback under a mutex without recreating the RX thread or resetting subscription state. Verified on EC2 with nrf52840dk/nrf52840 gateway build using CONFIG_LICHEN_NATIVE=y, compiling lichen/lib/native/native.c.
